div1.entryFree: Mace, a club of metal used as an ensign of authority: he that sets up his rest to do more exploits with his m. than a morris-pike, Err. IV, 3, 28. the sword, the m., the crown imperial, H5 IV, 1, 278 (== sceptre). with these borne before us instead of --s, H6B IV, 7, 144. O murderous slumber, layest thou thy leaden m. upon my boy? Caes. IV, 3, 268 (to arrest him like a bailiff; cf. Arrest).*
